description: User mentions their project lead by name in one session and the lead's native language in another. Probe asks what language the project lead speaks, requiring linking both facts.

messages.content: We kicked off the new project today. Yuki is leading it — she's been at the company for five years.
Sounds like Yuki has a lot of experience to bring to the project. What's the project about?
We're adding localization to the app. Carlos on the team speaks Spanish and Portuguese, so he's handling the Latin American strings.
That's great to have a native speaker on the localization work. Carlos covering Spanish and Portuguese is efficient.
Yuki is handling the Japanese localization herself since she's a native speaker. She grew up in Osaka.
Nice, having Yuki do the Japanese strings directly should give you high-quality translations.
We also need Mandarin support. I'm looking into hiring a contractor for that.
A contractor for Mandarin makes sense if nobody on the team covers it.
I want to check on the localization progress. Remind me, what language does my project lead speak natively?

probe.prompt: What language does my project lead speak natively?

interference.description: Carlos's languages (Spanish, Portuguese) and Mandarin mentioned for a contractor could be confused with Yuki's language
